question.text: Under PIS, what is the maximum investment limit for a single NRI in the paid-up capital of a listed Indian company?

answer.text: 10 per cent of the paid-up capital
15 per cent of the paid-up capital
5 per cent of the paid-up capital
3 per cent of the paid-up capital

explanation.text: **The Logic:** NRIs can invest through designated ADs, on repatriation and non-repatriation basis under PIS route, up to 5 per cent of the paid-up capital / paid-up value of each series of debentures of listed Indian companies. **The Trap:** The single NRI limit is 5%, while the aggregate NRI limit is 10% (which can be raised to 24% by board resolution + special resolution). For FPIs, the single limit is 10%. Candidates may confuse FPI and NRI limits. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember: NRI individual = 5%, NRI aggregate = 10% (extendable to 24%); FPI individual = 10%, FPI aggregate = 24% (extendable to sectoral cap).
